content.full_text:
Mr. John Wallace
Legal Section
FPPC
428 J St., Ste 800
Sacramento, Ca 95814

12/29/92

Dear Mr. Wallace:

This is to confirm the advice I received from you regarding the use of campaign funds by an officeholder. It is my understanding that a cellular telephone may be purchased by the Campaign to Reelect Sara Myers Committee from campaign funds if the use of the telephone is for officeholder duties. Furthermore, the monthly bills should be paid for by campaign funds. The officeholder may deposit a sufficient amount to cover the monthly bill if funds are low but the check must be from the campaign bank account.

Thank you very much for providing an interpretation of how this matter should be handled. A reply in writing would be appreciated for my file and should be sent to my home address:

Sara Myers
100 Baldwin Lake Circle
Folsom, Ca 95630.

For your information I was reelected to the Folsom City Council in Nov. 1992 and will serve a four year term. The phone is needed to communicate with the public on council business. Also, my employer doesn't want constituents or city staff calling me on the business telephone.

Sincerely,

Sara Myers

Sara Myers

916-988-8298 Home

sections.question_synthetic: Can campaign funds be used to purchase and maintain a cellular telephone for an officeholder's official duties?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming correspondence/follow-up letter from the requestor (Sara Myers) to FPPC, not an FPPC response letter. The letter confirms advice already received verbally and requests written confirmation for the requestor's files. No formal question or conclusion sections present. The advice content is paraphrased rather than in a formal response format.
